items.publisher-location: 400 Commonwealth Drive, Warrendale, PA, United States
items.abstract:
<jats:p><div class="section abstract">


<div class="htmlview paragraph">This SAE Recommended Practice describes the basic content requirements, bar-code specifications, and functional test specifications of a vehicle emission configuration (VEC) label. On the vehicle, the VEC label is to be mounted under the hood in a readily accessible location for use of a bar-code scanning device.</div>
</div></jats:p>
title: Recommended Practice for Bar-coded Vehicle Emission Configuration Label

items.publisher-location: 400 Commonwealth Drive, Warrendale, PA, United States
items.abstract:
<jats:p><p>This SAE Recommended Practice describes the basic content requirements, bar-code specifications, and functional test specifications of a vehicle emission configuration (VEC) label. On the vehicle, the VEC label is to be mounted under the hood in a readily accessible location for use of a bar-code scanning device.
</p>



<p>This document specifies a permanent vehicle emission configuration label that can be automatically identified through a bar-coded format.</p></jats:p>
title: Recommended Practice for Bar-Coded Vehicle Emission Configuration Label

title: RECOMMENDED PRACTICE FOR BAR-CODED VEHICLE EMISSION CONFIGURATION LABEL

items.publisher-location: 400 Commonwealth Drive, Warrendale, PA, United States
items.abstract:
<jats:p><div class="section abstract">


<div class="htmlview paragraph">SAE J1979-3 describes the communication between the zero emissions propulsion systems and test equipment required by government regulations. Standardization regulations require passenger cars and light-, medium-, and heavy-duty trucks to support a minimum set of diagnostic information to external (off-board) “generic” test equipment.</div>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">To achieve this, SAE J1979-3 is based on the Open Systems Interconnection (OSI) Basic Refer to Model in accordance with ISO/IEC 7498-1 and ISO/IEC 10731, which structures communication systems into seven layers. When mapped on this model, the services specified are broken into:</div>
<ul class="list disc">
<li class="list-item">
<div class="htmlview paragraph">Application (layer 7), specified in:
<ol class="list nostyle">
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">ISO 14229-1, ISO 14229-3 UDSonCAN, or ISO 14229-5 UDSonIP</div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">SAE J1979-3 ZEVonUDS</div></li></ol></div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<div class="htmlview paragraph">Presentation layer (layer 6), specified in:
<ol class="list nostyle">
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">SAE J1930, SAE J1930DA</div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">SAE J1979DA</div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">SAE J2012, SAE J2012DA</div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">SAE J1939DA, SAE J1939-73</div></li></ol></div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<div class="htmlview paragraph">Session layer services (layer 5), specified in:
<ol class="list nostyle">
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">ISO 14229-2</div></li></ol></div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<div class="htmlview paragraph">Transport layer services (layer 4), specified in:
<ol class="list nostyle">
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">DoCAN: ISO 15765-2 Transport protocol and network layer services</div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">ISO 15765-4 Requirements for emissions related systems</div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">DoIP: ISO 13400-2 Road vehicles - Diagnostic communication over Internet Protocol (DoIP) - Part 2: Transport protocol and network layer services</div></li></ol></div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<div class="htmlview paragraph">Network layer services (layer 3), specified in:
<ol class="list nostyle">
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">DoCAN: ISO 15765-2 Transport protocol and network layer services</div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">ISO 15765-4 Requirements for emissions related systems</div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">DoIP: ISO 13400-2 Road vehicles - Diagnostic communication over Internet Protocol (DoIP) - Part 2: Transport protocol and network layer services</div></li></ol></div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<div class="htmlview paragraph">Data link layer (layer 2), specified in:
<ol class="list nostyle">
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">DoCAN: ISO 15765-4, ISO 11898-1, -2</div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">DoIP: ISO 13400-3 Road vehicles - Diagnostic communication over Internet Protocol (DoIP) - Part 3: Wired vehicle interface based on IEEE 802.3</div></li></ol></div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<div class="htmlview paragraph">Physical layer (layer 1), specified in:
<ol class="list nostyle">
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">DoCAN: ISO 15765-4, ISO 11898-1, -2, in accordance with <span class="xref">Table 1</span></div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">DoIP: ISO 13400-3 Road vehicles - Diagnostic communication over Internet Protocol (DoIP) - Part 3: Wired vehicle interface based on IEEE 802.3</div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">ISO 13400-4 Road vehicles - Diagnostic communication over Internet Protocol (DoIP) - Part 4: Ethernet-based high-speed data link connector</div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">○</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">SAE J1962 Diagnostic Connector</div></li></ol></div></li></ul>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">NOTE: SAE J1979-3 provides a standard to satisfy known country-specific certification regulations. SAE J1979-3 is not considered a final authority for interpretation of the certification regulations, so users of this document should determine the applicability of capabilities defined in this document for their specific OBD regulatory requirements</div>



</div></jats:p>
title: E/E Diagnostic Test Modes: Zero Emission Vehicle Propulsion Systems on UDS (ZEVonUDS)

author.name: Vehicle E E System Diagnostic Standards Committee

items.publisher-location: 400 Commonwealth Drive, Warrendale, PA, United States
items.abstract:
<jats:p><div class="section abstract">


<div class="htmlview paragraph">This document is intended to satisfy the data reporting requirements of standardization regulations in the United States and Europe, and any other market that may adopt similar requirements in the future. This document specifies:</div>
<ol class="list nostyle">
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">a</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">Message formats for request and response messages.</div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">b</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">Timing requirements between request messages from external test equipment and response messages from vehicles, and between those messages and subsequent request messages.</div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">c</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">Behavior of both the vehicle and external test equipment if data is not available.</div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">d</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">A set of diagnostic services, with corresponding content of request and response messages.</div></li>
<li class="list-item">
<span class="li-label">e</span>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">Standardized source and target addresses for clients and vehicle.</div></li></ol>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">This document includes capabilities required to satisfy OBD requirements for multiple regions, model years, engine types, and vehicle types. At the time of publication many regional regulations are not yet final and are expected to change in the future. This document makes no attempt to interpret the regulations and does not include applicability of the included diagnostic services and data parameters for various vehicle applications. The user of this document is responsible to verify the applicability of each section of this document for a specific vehicle, engine, model year, and region.</div>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">SAE J1979-3 specifies diagnostic services, and both functionally addressed and physically addressed request/response messages required to be supported by motor vehicles and external test equipment for diagnostic purposes which pertain to motor vehicle emission-related data. Any external test equipment meeting the requirements of SAE J1978 is intended to be able to use these messages to retrieve ZEV propulsion-related information from the vehicle.</div>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">The structure of this document is based on the structure of SAE J1979 for the convenience of users familiar with SAE J1979. Each section of this part of SAE J1979-3 which specifies additional detail to existing sections of ISO 15765-4 and SAE J1979 supersedes those specifications for their use under the scope of SAE J1979-2. When applicable, a reference to respective sections in SAE J1979-2 is made in this document.</div>
<div class="htmlview paragraph">SAE J1979-3 references SAE J1979DA, which includes all Data Identifiers and Routine Identifiers.</div></div></jats:p>
title: E/E Diagnostic Test Modes: Zero Emission Vehicle Propulsion Systems on UDS (ZEVonUDS)

author.name: Vehicle E E System Diagnostic Standards Committee
